The fact that they keep reviving these old popular franchises only gives the audience the opportunity to do a direct 1:1 comparison and see the glaring ways in which the previous generation better understood storytelling. 

It sounds pretentious, but these professional writers need to sit down and actually read old literature - the western canon classics. Our civilization has been producing epic, beautiful and moving stories for millennia and yet consistently it seems like the people who populate the industry today are unfamiliar with any of it. It makes everything they write come off as fan fiction. 

The unfortunate part is that in other respects this medium is at it's pinnacle - the visual and sound design you get in a lot of modern stuff is spectacular, but it all feels pretty empty if the stories and characters driving it are empty anf trite.
